REPLY DECLARATION OF DEPUTY CITY ATTY JERRY THREET IN SUPPORT OF PLTF'S TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER AND PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION (VOL 1 OF 3) FILED BY PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL CORPORATION PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, BY AND THROUGH DENNIS J. HERRERA, CITY ATTORNEY FOR THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO

LAW AND MOTION 302, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE REGARDING PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED IN PART, DENIED IN PART. ALL REQUESTED INJUNCTIVE RELIEF IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS HAVE SHOWN THAT THEY ARE LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON THE MERITS OF THEIR DRUG ABATEMENT ACT CLAIM AND THAT BOTH THE PUBLIC INTEREST AND THE BALANCE OF HARDSHIPS FAVORS GRANTING APPROPRIATELY TAILORED PRELIMINARY INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. (HEALTH AND SAFETY CODE) AS TO THE REQUESTED RELIEF PROHIBITING SENDING CERTAIN MONEY OUT OF STATE, THE RELIEF IS DENIED. PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT SHOWN THE NEED TO PROHIBIT SENDING MONEY OUT OF STATE IS NOT SUPPORTED BY THE EVIDENCE. STATUS COMPLIANCE HEARING IS SET FOR 3/20/12 AT 11:30 A.M. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR 7077

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Mar-20-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jerry J. Threet, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3914, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party.
Jose Luis Fuentes, Esq., of Siegel & Yee, Tel. 510.839.1200, appearing for Defendant and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction. The court grants in part and denies in part plaintiff's request for preliminary injunction without prejudice. Amended order is required to clear the errors. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-06-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jose Luis Fuentes, Esq., Siegel & Yee, 510-839-1200, for Defendant and moving party
Jerry J. Threet, DCA, Office of the City Attorney, 415-554-3914, for Plaintiff and opposing party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling:
DEFENDANT JABER ALGAHIM'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Overruled. All three claims are sufficiently pled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077

Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Mar-06-2012 Continued To Mar-20-12 At 11:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jerry J. Threet, DCA, Office of the City Attorney, 415-554-3914, for Plaintiff and moving party
Jose Luis Fuentes, Esq., Siegel & Yee, 510-839-1200, for Defendant and opposing party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:
Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction. Granted in part, denied in part. All requested injunctive relief is granted.
Plaintiffs have shown that they are likely to prevail on the merits of their Drug Abatement Act claim and that both the public interest and the balance of hardships favors granting appropriately tailored preliminary injunctive relief (Health and Safety Code)
As to The requested relief prohibiting sending certain money out of state, the relief is denied. Plaintiffs have not shown the need to prohibit sending money out of state is not supported by the evidence.
Status Compliance hearing is set for 3/20/12 at 11:30 a.m. Order signed in open court.
Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077
